rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE NIKOLIS BALDERAS SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #7, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/28/2020. 1) The current bond appears insufficient based on the ending fair market value and receipts, plus statutory recovery costs. An ex parte petition for an order increasing the bond must be filed. 2) Please identify which persons listed in paragraph 9 are contingent beneficiaries of the trust. 3) Please provide a detailed schedule of loan payments omitted from the 2nd account. Schedule 2 alleges that the 2nd account period ended on 07/31/2010, but the correct ending date appears to be 04/30/2010. 4) What public benefits is the beneficiary currently receiving? Did any reported disbursements, allowances for living expenses in particular, affect the beneficiary's entitlement to the public benefits? 5) A copy of a certificate of title to the Chevrolet Tahoe title showing the trust's lienholder status has not been filed. 6) Are the money market fund and other securities held in the First Republic account authorized investments under Probate Code section 2574(a)?

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JULIA CASEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance highly encouraged. Please review the 7th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. Issues: 1) Matter set for hearing because the attorney Declaration not signed or verified by petitioner does not adequately address issue 1a. Examiner sent the following notes: a. Petitions need to include sufficient allegations to allow the Court to determine administration is proceeding properly. Please make all necessary allegations as if the petition was for final distribution, including but not limited to: Notice under Probate Code 9202; Creditor's Claims; Assignments/Powers of Attorney; Requests for Special Notice, etc. 2) A hard copy of the proposed order must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. (LRSF 14.93). [Proposed orders may not be e-filed.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FRANZ VON UCKERMANN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Reinstatement Of Suspended Executors
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 7th Amended General Order which took effect on 10/30/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CARY HAMMER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing for the purpose of admitting the will dated 12/29/2018. No appearance required, although notice of this hearing was mailed to Milo Hammer at an address that was not set forth in any verified pleading. Status of closing the estate will be set on 6/7/2022. A petition for final distribution or a report of status with a petition requesting additional time for estate administration must be filed at least one week before 6/7/2022. Appearance will be required on 6/7/2022 if no petition has been filed.

Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. A copy of the account statement from September 2012 was filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LOIS MAY GINTJEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO DETERMINE SUCCESSION TO REAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. This matter will be heard with PES-20-303984. Hearing issues have been e-mailed to counsel. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. ***Until further notice, the Court strongly encourages remote appearances by video or telephone. Counsel and parties may appear by video or audio-only telephone through Zoom. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Review the Court's 7th Amended General Order which took effect on 10/30/2020 for more information. If counsel or any party appears in person, he or she should anticipate delays at the entrance to the courthouse and inside the courthouse. Masks must be worn at all times while in the courthouse and social distancing protocols will be strictly enforced, including inside the courtroom. Anyone making an in-person appearance must first familiarize themselves with the Court's current pandemic-related requirements and protocols by reviewing the Court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/.
